added_paragraphs: Your privacy is important. As of 13 April 2026 we’ve updated our Privacy Policy to include additional detail about our data processing.
You can edit your User Data under ‘Edit profile’ in your account or by contacting us. |

You can exercise your right to opt out through the ‘Tailored Ads’ setting. If you do not have an account or are not logged in, you can also opt-out by clicking the 'Your Privacy Choices' link at the footer of our website. |

Age Check Data is the data you provide if you (i) choose to use a feature that is subject to an Age Check or (ii) are a parent or legal guardian providing parental consent for a Managed Account (“Age Check”). Examples of Age Check methods we use, which are powered by a third party provider, are:
All Age Check Data is deleted immediately after the Age Check. |
You can also watch our video about Personal Data at Spotify.
For marketing or advertising purposes. For example:
You can see and remove many third party connections under ‘Apps’ in your account. |

To receive news or promotional offers from artists, record labels or other partners. You may choose to share your User Data for this purpose when you sign up to the Spotify Service. You’ll always have the option to change your mind and withdraw your consent at any time. |

Courts and Authorities: We may process and share your personal data to comply with a request from courts, authorities, parties to litigation, or other authorized third parties. We will do so when we believe in good faith it is necessary, including:
- to respond to a valid legal process (such as a search warrant, court order, or subpoena)
- for our own or a third party's justifiable interest such as criminal investigations, litigation, protecting someone’s safety, or preventing death or imminent bodily harm
The categories of personal data processed or shared will depend on the nature of the specific request.
The Spotify Service (outside of Managed Accounts or Spotify Kids) that is tied to this privacy policy has a minimum age limit in each country or region (the “Age Limit”).

If you’re under the Age Limit, do not create or use the standard Spotify Service. Instead, we recommend asking a parent or guardian to create a Managed Account for you, where available. If you are above the Age Limit but under the age of majority in your country (a “Minor”), you may use the Spotify Service only with the consent of your parent or legal guardian.
llm_summary: Spotify updated its Privacy Policy to provide more details about data processing, including options for users to edit their data and opt out of tailored advertising. The policy clarifies the handling of Age Check Data and emphasizes that all such data is deleted immediately after use. Additionally, there are new sections regarding compliance with legal requests and the minimum age requirements for using the service.

modified_paragraphs.after: - This is when we use information about your use of our services and other websites and mobile apps to tailor ads to be more relevant to you. This is also known as interest based advertising, targeted advertising, or ‘sharing’ for purposes of cross-context behavioral advertising.
modified_paragraphs.before: - This is when we use information about your use of our services and other websites and mobile apps to tailor ads to be more relevant to you. This is also known as interest based advertising, targeted advertising, or ‘sharing’ for purposes of cross-context behavioural advertising.

modified_paragraphs.after: - You can control tailored advertising through the ‘Tailored Ads’ setting.
modified_paragraphs.before: - You can control tailored advertising on your Account Privacy page under ‘Tailored Ads’.

modified_paragraphs.after: If you are ‘opted out’ of tailored advertising, you will receive less relevant advertising, including on our free Service Option, as well as our paid Service Option, as applicable (for example, advertising in podcasts). This type of advertising is based on your registration information and what you are currently listening to on our services. For example, if you are listening to a cooking podcast, you may hear an ad for a food processor.
modified_paragraphs.before: If you are ‘opted out’ of tailored advertising on your Account Privacy page, you will receive less relevant advertising, including on our free Service Option, as well as our paid Service Option, as applicable (for example, advertising in podcasts). This type of advertising is based on your registration information and what you are currently listening to on our services. For example, if you are listening to a cooking podcast, you may hear an ad for a food processor.

modified_paragraphs.after: - Younger users may have tailored advertising turned off by default. When you reach the appropriate age, you will start to receive tailored advertising. When this happens, you will receive a message within the Spotify Service to let you know. This message will contain a link to the Tailored Ads setting where you can change your preferences or ‘opt-out’ of tailored advertising at any time.
modified_paragraphs.before: - Younger users may have tailored advertising turned off by default. When you reach the appropriate age, you will start to receive tailored advertising . When this happens, you will receive a message within the Spotify Service to let you know. This message will contain a link to the Account Privacy page where you can change your preferences or ‘opt-out’ of tailored advertising at any time under Tailored Ads.

modified_paragraphs.before: If we direct you to a merchant, we receive data from the merchant that is related to your purchase. For example, we might direct you to an artist’s merchandise store on a third party platform or to a third party ticketing website.

modified_paragraphs.after: To conduct business planning, reporting, and forecasting. For example, when we look at aggregated user data like the number of new sign ups in a country in order to plan new locations to launch our products and features in. |
modified_paragraphs.before: For example, when we look at aggregated user data like the number of new sign ups in a country in order to plan new locations to launch our products and features in.

Shared playlists give you social recommendations based on your listening activity. |

modified_paragraphs.after: Hosting platforms host podcasts so that they can deliver them to you. modified_paragraphs.after: Note: This Policy doesn't apply to Managed Accounts or Spotify Kids except where those respective privacy notices say so.
modified_paragraphs.before: Note: This Policy doesn't apply to Spotify Kids except where the Spotify Kids Privacy Policy says so. Spotify Kids is a separate Spotify application.

modified_paragraphs.after: We do not knowingly collect or use personal data from children under the applicable Age Limit outside of Managed Accounts or Spotify Kids. If you are a parent or guardian and become aware that your child, who is under the application Age Limit, has provided personal data to Spotify in any other context, contact us. If we learn that we’ve collected the personal data of a child under the applicable Age Limit, we’ll take reasonable steps to delete the personal data. This may require us to delete the Spotify account for that child.
modified_paragraphs.before: If we learn that we’ve collected the personal data of a child under the applicable Age Limit, we’ll take reasonable steps to delete the personal data. This may require us to delete the Spotify account for that child.

removed_paragraphs: Please note there are situations where Spotify is unable to delete your data, for example when:
For more information about our processing for these purposes, see the section ‘Tailored advertising controls’ below.
You can do this if Spotify is processing your personal data solely based on your consent.
Personal data that we need to create your Spotify account and that enables you to use the Spotify Service.
We receive some of this data from you e.g. from the sign up form or account page.
There are a few types of information this includes, listed in the following sections.
Age Check Data is the data you provide if you use an Age Check powered by a third party provider on Spotify (“Age Check”). This includes:

Where Age Check Data is collected, we will obtain your consent and provide notice of the third party policies that apply to the processing of such data. All Age Check Data is deleted immediately after the Age Check.
The exact personal data collected and used will vary depending on the payment method. It will include information such as:
These third party apps, services or devices may include:
This makes it possible for Spotify to provide the Spotify Service, content, and features.
We also work with security service providers who help us protect user accounts.
This allows us to deliver more relevant ads and marketing.
• provide the Spotify app when you download it onto your device,
• enable you to share a link to Spotify content with someone else, or
• provide an age-appropriate experience, or
• provide various optional features of the Spotify Service
• to help us develop and improve our personalized recommendations
• to help analyze how our users react to a particular new feature and see whether we should make any changes
• so we can build AI features such as AI DJ and AI playlist
• to help us enforce our User Guidelines, Platform Rules and Spotify for Creators Community Guidelines.
• when we use your personal data to tailor advertising to your interests, or
• an obligation under the law of the country / region you are in
• Swedish law (because of our headquarters in Sweden), or
To comply with a request from law enforcement, courts, or other competent authorities.
To fulfill contractual obligations with third parties.
Pseudonymized data means that your data is identified by a code rather than your name or other directly identifying information.
To establish, exercise, or defend legal claims.
To conduct business planning, reporting, and forecasting.
For example, when we analyze Usage Data to check for fraudulent use of the Spotify Service.
Examples of such third party applications, services and devices include:
To share information about your use of the Spotify Service with other Spotify users. These could include your followers on Spotify.
To receive news or promotional offers from artists, record labels or other partners.
These service providers include those we hire to:
• operate the technical infrastructure we need to provide the Spotify Service
• assist in protecting and securing our systems and services (e.g. Google’s reCAPTCHA)
• help market Spotify’s (and our partners’) products, services, events and promotions
• verify your eligibility for certain types of Service Options (e.g., Spotify Premium Student)
For example, our ad partners help us facilitate tailored advertising.
With respect to Spotify Service users under the age of 18, we do not disclose personal data to advertising partners by default.
• enable you to participate in Spotify promotions, including trials or other bundled offers
• to promote Spotify in media and advertising published on other online services
• help us and our partners to measure the effectiveness of Spotify promotions
• websites and mobile apps who sell us advertising space on their services
• device, app and mobile partners who also offer Spotify promotions
With respect to Spotify Service users under the age of 18, we do not disclose personal data to marketing partners by default.
Podcast providers should explain in the show or episode description which platform is hosting the podcast. See the hosting platform’s own privacy policy for how they use data disclosed to them.
• enabling our employees who work for different group companies to develop and improve features for the Spotify Service
• disclosing data to our measurement companies to measure the effectiveness of ad campaigns that run on the Spotify Service
• disclosing data to our podcast companies to better understand user listening trends
Law enforcement and other authorities, or other parties to litigation
When we believe in good faith it’s necessary for us to do so, for example:
In this case, we may transfer your personal data to a successor or affiliate as part of that transaction.
This helps us investigate potential breaches of our User Guidelines and Platform Rules. On the other hand, we will remove unlawful content if the law requires us to do so.
The Spotify Service has a minimum ‘Age Limit’ in each country or region. The Spotify Service is not directed to children whose age:
- makes it illegal to process their personal data, or
- requires parental consent to process their personal data

We do not knowingly collect or use personal data from children under the applicable Age Limit. If you’re under the Age Limit, do not use the Spotify Service, and do not provide any personal data to us. Instead, we recommend using a Spotify Kids account.
If you’re a parent of a child under the Age Limit and become aware that your child has provided personal data to Spotify, contact us.
